claim: AMS MMONO/223 'Painlevé Equations through Symmetry' by Masatoshi Noumi (Kobe Univ.) is 156 pages, year 2004, DOI 10.1090/mmono/223, eBook ISBN 978-1-4704-4647-5, MSC primary 34M55, $155 paywall on AMS Bookstore.

AMS MMONO/223 digital TOC enumerates exactly 8 chapters, none dedicated to Painlevé III: (1) What is a Bäcklund transformation? pp.1-12; (2) The symmetric form pp.13-25; (3) τ-functions pp.27-43; (4) τ-functions on the lattice pp.45-66; (5) Jacobi-Trudi formula pp.67-83; (6) Getting familiar with determinants pp.85-97; (7) Gauss decomposition and birational transformations pp.99-124; (8) Lax formalism pp.125-132.

AMS abstract for MMONO/223 explicitly states the worked examples are 'especially those of types II and IV' (i.e., W(\tilde A_1) and W(\tilde A_2) — simply-laced A-type), confirming PIII = W(B_2) / W((2A_1)^(1)) is not a featured example in the book.

Three NY-family Tier-2 OA preprints acquired and SHA-256 hashed: slot 14 = arXiv:1509.08186 KNY 2017 'Geometric Aspects of Painlevé Equations' (168 pp, J.Phys.A 50 073001) sha256=7CD884617FC8F17E430DEAF0520148362E1871CAFB8F8471B462029CBE387DDE; slot 15 = arXiv:math/9804132 NY 1998 'Affine Weyl groups, discrete dynamical systems and Painlevé equations' (16 pp, CMP 199, 281-295) sha256=32424FE6CC4FCEC386A0D2C1733F40CDED52AE5E295AC344DC8B5E2A79F58524; slot 16 = arXiv:math/0012028 NY 2000 'Birational Weyl group action arising from a nilpotent Poisson algebra' (31 pp, MathPhys Odyssey 2001 pp.287-319) sha256=75698AFB6AF9D593DEA674CACF1CA6F40CFCC067E29941ACD17472754E4EAAF3.

KNY 2017 (slot 14, 168 pp) contains 0 occurrences of 'B_2' / 'B(2)' / 'B_{2}' as a Lie-group symbol; the only grep hit for 'B 2' is citation [7] Brézin-Kazakov 'Phys. Lett. B 236' (a journal name). The book does cover Sakai-style W((2A_1)^(1))/D_6^(1), W((A_1)^(1))/D_7^(1), W(0)/D_8^(1) framings for PIII6 / PIII7 / PIII8 (sec.8.6.16 ff.).

NY 1998 (slot 15, 16 pp) explicitly lists Painlevé equations P_II / P_III / P_IV / P_V / P_VI as admitting affine Weyl group symmetries (line 25) but develops only the A_l^(1) construction in the body. There is no specialization to PIII / W(B_2) / W((2A_1)^(1)) and no W(B_2) ↔ W((2A_1)^(1)) homomorphism statement.

NY 2000 (slot 16, 31 pp) develops the abstract Weyl-group → birational-canonical-transformation framework for arbitrary Kac-Moody type W. The worked example is W(G_2) (line 426). There is no specialization to PIII or to W(B_2) → W((2A_1)^(1)).

PIII / PIII' chapter location in NY 2004: NOT FOUND. The book has no chapter/section dedicated to PIII; PIII may be referenced in passing within the unified τ-function or symmetric-form chapters but cannot be the primary worked example given the AMS abstract's 'especially II and IV' claim. Phase A audit trail: the AMS digital TOC was the basis for this finding.

Spec §1 Tier-2 list contains a typo: arXiv:math/9806032 was identified as 'Symmetries in PIV / Okamoto polynomials' but math/9806032 is actually Schlichenmaier's 'Sugawara Construction for Higher Genus Riemann Surfaces' (Rept.Math.Phys. 43, 323-339, 1999). The correct arXiv ID for NY 'Symmetries in PIV / Okamoto polynomials' (Nagoya Math.J. 153 (1999) 53-86) is q-alg/9708018 (1997-08-19, 25 pp). Substantive impact: zero (PIV-focused, not the PIII bridge anyway).

Across all 3 acquired NY-family OA sources (slots 14/15/16), the explicit W(B_2) ↔ W((2A_1)^(1)) homomorphism (or isomorphism / quotient / covering) is NOT stated. KNY 2017 cites Okamoto's W-symmetry observation but does not retrace the historical W(B_2) → W((2A_1)^(1)) translation; NY 1998/2000 do not specialize to PIII at all.

M6 spec Phase B.5 anchor-grade upgrade recommendation: NO_UPGRADE_FROM_NY_FAMILY_OA. Anchor remains anchor-PARTIAL. Recommended pathways to close: (1) ILL of NY 2004 (operator-side, libraries: Yokohama City Library, University of Tokyo Math Lib, Kobe University Math Lib, RIMS Kyoto); (2) follow-on Researcher prompt for Witte-Forrester 2010 (J.Phys.A 43, 235202) τ-function PIII anchor; (3) Researcher / SIARC primary derivation of the B_2 → (2A_1)^(1) homomorphism from Cartan-matrix data (folklore Lie theory; produces citable statement under SIARC authorship).

Phase D substitution checks D.3 (NY 2004 W(B_2) vs Okamoto §2.1) and D.4 (NY 2004 W((2A_1)^(1)) vs Sakai Table 6) are vacuous because NY 2004 paywall was not breached and the Tier-2 surrogates do not contain PIII W-generators. No discrepancy_log.json entry warranted; halt-conditions §4 not triggered.

G17 (CT v1.4 §3.5 amendment language) anchor count unchanged. The 3 sources cited in 2026-05-04 G17-LAYER-SEPARATION-LIT-ANCHOR (B-T 1933 + Costin 2008 + Okamoto 1987 + BLMP 2024) are not augmented by NY 2004 / KNY 2017 since neither contains an explicit layer-separation framing. KNY 2017 sec 8 works in the surface-classification framework which is geometrically equivalent but is not phrased as a layer separation.
